name: DNS Exfiltration in Powershell
notes:
This is rule detects DNS exfiltration in powershell.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113
SIGMA Title: Powershell DNSExfiltration
SIGMA Description: DNSExfiltrator allows for transferring (exfiltrate) a file over a DNS request covert channel


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Exploit.Command Execution

when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%invoke-dnsexfiltrator%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -i %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -d %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -p %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -doh %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -t %' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY DNS Exfiltration in Powershell on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Exploit.Command Execution

AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%invoke-dnsexfiltrator%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -i %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -d %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -p %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -doh %' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -t %' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: DNS Exfiltration in Powershell
Event description: Detects DNS exfiltration in powershell.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
